summary: SealTech Books Phase 1 (Entities + Chart of Accounts) tested end-to-end. 11/11 pytest backend tests passed and all 12 UI flows from the review request validated successfully. Default seed: 4 entities + 44 accounts each. SYSTEM account protection (lock number/type, block delete), CONTRA flag display, duplicate guards, soft-delete, and localStorage entity persistence all behave correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: DELETE /api/books/entities does a soft-deactivate (is_active=false) but the default list filter is_active=true means deactivated entities silently disappear from the UI with no way to view/restore them from the switcher — consider adding a 'show inactive' toggle on the BooksCOA page (the backend already supports include_inactive=true).

EntityIn.is_active defaults to True; PUT /api/books/entities/{id} with a partial body will re-set is_active=true on every edit (because Pydantic fills defaults), so editing a soft-deleted entity will silently reactivate it. Consider using a PATCH model or making is_active immutable through this endpoint.

Frontend localStorage key 'books_entity_id' is not invalidated if the saved id no longer exists (e.g. entity was deactivated). Add a fallback to first available entity when the stored id is not present in the loaded entities list.

books.py: chart_of_accounts unique index is on (entity_id, number); since soft-deleted accounts keep their number, a user cannot create a new account that reuses a deactivated number — minor but worth documenting.

Account update endpoint allows changing entity_id on non-system accounts (the payload includes entity_id and the dup-check uses update['entity_id']) — likely should be locked server-side, since moving an account between entities corrupts ledgers later.
No size limit on bulk listing — GET /api/books/accounts has no pagination, fine for 44 default rows but should be considered if accounts grow.
seed_data_creation: test_books.py creates TEST_QA_Entity_* (deactivated at end of test) and one TEST_QA_Account (soft-deleted at end). UI test left one 'QA Test Entity {timestamp}' entity active in DB — harmless; can be removed manually via DELETE /api/books/entities/{id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Books Phase 1 backend file: /app/backend/books.py — seed_default_entities() runs on startup hook and is idempotent. Tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_books.py and are pytest-style with admin login fixture. Frontend page: /app/frontend/src/pages/BooksCOA.jsx, route /books, nav link data-testid='nav-books'. Note: entity-switcher uses a native select but options may not enumerate via .options in Playwright until the React state has fully hydrated — using select_option by index works reliably.
